content.text:
<user_query>
I have a script that automatically causes the Terminal to have a short prompt. It worked for a bit, but does not work now. 
</user_query>

Searching the repo and common config locations for what controls your short terminal prompt.

[REDACTED]

Identifying a mismatch: the default terminal profile references a profile that isn't defined.

[REDACTED]

Updating the default terminal profile: `terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.windows` is set to `"PowerShell (Memories prompt)"`, but only `"PowerShell (short path)"` is defined — VS Code falls back to the stock PowerShell profile, so `powershell-terminal-layout.ps1` never runs.

[REDACTED]

Here is what was going wrong.

### Cause

In `.vscode/settings.json` you define a terminal profile named **`PowerShell (short path)`**, which dot-sources `scripts/powershell-terminal-layout.ps1` and sets the short prompt.

You also had:

```json
"terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.windows": "PowerShell (Memories prompt)"
```

There is **no** profile with that exact name in the same file. When the default name does not match any profile, the editor uses its normal PowerShell startup, so your layout script is never loaded and the long default prompt stays.

That often happens after a rename: one string was updated, the other was not.

### Fix applied

`terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.windows` is now set to **`PowerShell (short path)`**, so new terminals use the profile that runs your script.

**What you should do:** Close existing terminals (or open a new one); the integrated terminal picks up defaults per new terminal.

**Alternative:** If you prefer the label “Memories prompt”, rename the profile key in JSON from `"PowerShell (short path)"` to `"PowerShell (Memories prompt)"` and set `defaultProfile` to that same string so they stay identical.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
Can you sync this update to the cursor-template repo in the same parent folder, which is a generalized template for this and future projects
</user_query>

Checking the cursor-template repo's VS Code terminal settings and script so we can align them with Memories.

[REDACTED]

Verifying the two files match exactly:

[REDACTED]

input.description: Binary compare Memories vs cursor-template VS Code/script files

input.command: cd "c:\Users\Ken Levy\OneDrive\Documents\Business\Ohana\cursor-template" && git status --short -- .vscode/settings.json scripts/powershell-terminal-layout.ps1 && git diff -- .vscode/settings.json
input.description: Check git status of synced files in cursor-template

input.command: Set-Location "c:\Users\Ken Levy\OneDrive\Documents\Business\Ohana\cursor-template"; git status --short -- .vscode/settings.json scripts/powershell-terminal-layout.ps1; git diff -- .vscode/settings.json
input.description: Git status and diff for template terminal config
Verify cursor-template is a git repo and show branch status

content.text:
Here is what verification shows:

### Already in sync

`fc /b` reports **no differences** between:

- `Memories\.vscode\settings.json` and `cursor-template\.vscode\settings.json`
- `Memories\scripts\powershell-terminal-layout.ps1` and `cursor-template\scripts\powershell-terminal-layout.ps1`

Both use `terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.windows` set to **`PowerShell (short path)`**, matching the profile Block that dot-sources `${workspaceFolder}/scripts/powershell-terminal-layout.ps1`.

In `cursor-template`, those paths are clean in git (`git status` showed no changes).

So there was nothing left to copy: the template on disk already has the same fix as Memories after your update.

### If it still breaks in another project

Copy **both**:

1. `.vscode/settings.json` (or at least the `terminal.integrated.*` entries), and  
2. `scripts/powershell-terminal-layout.ps1`.

Also ensure **`defaultProfile`** is exactly the **same string** as the profile **key** under `terminal.integrated.profiles.windows`; a typo or rename there is what broke Memories before.

`cursor-template` is **ahead of `origin/master` by 1 commit** locally; pushing that branch will publish whatever is already queued there ( unrelated to these two files if they are unchanged).

[REDACTED]
